what_it_does: Renders the 'grow' region UI: a form to teach a new node, a surfaced proposal card for operator approval/rejection, a lane of live node-type chips, and a footer to revert the last self-change.
observations: Uses shared kit primitives (SectionHead, LaneHead, Badge, Surface) for a consistent visual language.
Preserves data-ui-ref attributes such as ui://grow, ui://grow/dispatch, ui://grow/approve, ui://grow/reject, and ui://workshop/self-changes.
Surfaces proposals as a kit Surface with tone='await', including a plain-language COA and an optional raw-code drill-down.
Build errors are shown as a kit Surface with tone='fail'.
Live node-types are rendered as a kit lane of dim-toned Badge chips with a count.
The last self-change is shown as a dim kit Surface with a bounded revert button.
Approval is operator-initiated via approveApply; rejection calls api.resolve inline and clears the surfaced state.
The file is described as a pure markup pass with no new state or controller changes.
standouts: Operator-only approval flow is preserved and emphasized: the brain never self-applies.
Proposal state is communicated through kit Surface tones (await/fail/dim) rather than ad-hoc styling.
The surfaced proposal supports a COA plain-language draft plus an optional raw-code drill-down.
conventions: Functional React component exported by name.
State and callbacks are pulled from a single useApp() context hook.
data-ui-ref values follow a ui://... URI scheme.
Conditional rendering uses short-circuit && and ternary expressions.
concerns: The reject handler is an inline anonymous function that mixes UI state updates, API call, and polling in JSX.
No visible loading, disabled, or error-boundary handling for the async approve/reject/dispatch actions.
The reason input is rendered for both approve and reject, but it is unclear from this file whether approveApply consumes it.
The shape and guarantees of surf, lastChange, and types are not defined in this file.
notes: A markup-only refactor of the grow region into shared kit components while keeping operator-controlled proposal approval and rejection intact.
questions: Does approveApply use the reason value, or is reason only captured on rejection?
What is the exact shape of the surf object and what guarantees does it provide?
What does poll() do and how is it expected to behave after reject?
Where are types and lastChange populated and validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it redesigns the grow region using the shared kit while preserving operator approval, data-ui-refs, and the reject path.
apparent_themes: Proposal review as a first-class card UI
Registry truth as a navigable chip lane
intention_signals: It tries to present AI-generated code changes as structured, color-coded proposal cards that clearly await a human verdict, while keeping teaching, registry inspection, and last-change audit in one coherent region.
novelty: The use of kit Surface tones (await/fail/dim) to encode proposal lifecycle state and the conversion of a comma-separated type list into a counted lane of kind-toned chips.
contribution: A self-contained region component that wires AppContext state into a structured proposal-review UI, providing operator approve/reject affordances, a live node-type registry display, and a bounded last-change revert, all anchored by traceable data-ui-ref markers.
summary_for_embedding: Grow.tsx is a React region component that renders the 'grow' UI for teaching nodes, reviewing surfaced code proposals, listing live node-types, and reverting the last self-change using a shared kit component vocabulary.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to refactor the grow region into a consistent commander's-bridge interface using shared kit components while keeping operator-controlled approval and rejection flows intact.

symbols.does: Renders the grow region UI for teaching a new node, reviewing surfaced proposals, listing live node-types, and reverting the last self-change.
symbols.description: The Grow component consumes AppContext state and callbacks to present a teaching form, a surfaced proposal card with operator approve/reject actions, a lane of live node-type chips, and a dim audit surface for reverting the last self-change. It relies on shared kit primitives (SectionHead, Surface, Badge, LaneHead) and preserves operator-initiated control flows and data-ui-ref markers.
